column7:
1. Concepts: AI vs. BI, difference between descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics.
2. Industry applications: finance (automated risk alerts), retail (dynamic pricing), supply chain (demand forecasting).
3. AI-Powered Data Analytics Tools
Tools: ChatGPT (data summarization), Gemini for exploratory analysis, Microsoft Copilot/Google Sheets AI.
4. Descriptive & Diagnostic Analytics with AI
 • Using AI for descriptive analytics → summarize datasets automatically
 • Using AI for diagnostic analytics → finding “why” behind anomalies
 • Using AI to create charts
5. Multimodal Analytics (including text & sentiment analysis)
 • OCR (image → text) for reports/document extraction
 • Speech-to-text for meeting summaries
 • Image pattern extraction

column5: Simulation: Applying AI to the Dataset
column6: Week 7 - Simulation: Applying AI to the Dataset
column7:
1.	Apply 1–2 lightweight AI analytics techniques to the capstone dataset (e.g., simple prediction signal, sentiment/topic extraction, prioritization score).
	2.	Identify one insight/output that is most valuable to operationalize (the “must-build” use case).
	3.	Convert the chosen use case into Workflow Blueprint v1:
	•	trigger/event
	•	required input data fields
	•	AI step and expected output format
	•	branch/decision rule
	•	final destination/action (Slack/Email/Sheet/CRM)
	•	human review/approval checkpoint
	4.	Define 3 test scenarios (normal, edge, failure) and expected results.
	5.	Present blueprint for peer/facilitator checkpoint and scope lock for Week 8 implementation.
column8:
•	Apply basic AI analytics to identify a practical automation opportunity from capstone data.
	•	Select and justify one high-impact use case to automate based on business value and feasibility.
	•	Produce a build-ready workflow blueprint with clear logic, decision rules, and output path.
	•	Create a test plan that prepares the team for Week 8 implementation and validation.

column7:
1. Fundamentals of AI Driven Solutions
2. From Workflow to Product – UX Foundations
- Explain why UX is critical for adoption of AI-driven analytics & automation.
- Identify the primary user persona (role, goals, pains) for the capstone solution.
- Map how the existing assistant + workflows fit into a simple end-to-end user journey (what the user sees first, does next, and gets as outcome).
3. UI Patterns for Data & AI
- Recognise common UI patterns for AI solutions (dashboard + AI panel, triage inbox, approval screen, chat + context).
- Match at least one pattern to their own capstone use case (e.g., complaint triage, lead qualification, performance monitoring).
- Describe how AI insights and automation results will be surfaced in the interface (where summaries, alerts, and actions appear).
5. Capstone project sharing
 • Upgrade automation to include a simple agent behavior (decision rule, routing, or multi-step reasoning in n8n)
 • Test end-to-end with realistic scenario
column8:

• Produce a concise UX mini pack (persona, user flow, 2–3 wireframes) that can be reused directly in the final pitch. 
• Integrate basic responsible-AI considerations (privacy, human review, error states) into their UX and translate this into a concrete build plan for the remaining weeks.
